title: shorea ovalis subsp. sericea
intro: Shorea ovalis subsp. sericea, also known as Seraya, is a plant species native to the tropical forests of Southeast Asia, belonging to the family Dipterocarpaceae. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which have been attributed to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic compounds. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, such as reducing fever and alleviating respiratory issues, as well as its use in treating wounds and skin conditions. Seraya can be prepared into various herbal remedies, including decoctions, infusions, and ointments, which are often used in traditional medicine to promote healing and well-being.
